content.problemStatement:
A binary string SSS is said to be good if it can be converted into a palindrome†^\dagger† by performing the following operation several (possibly zero) times:


Choose two adjacent equal characters in SSS and delete them.

Formally, choose an index iii (1≤i<∣S∣1 \leq i \lt |S|1≤i<∣S∣) such that Si=Si+1S_i = S_{i+1}Si​=Si+1​, and delete SiS_iSi​ and Si+1S_{i+1}Si+1​ from SSS, without changing the order of the other elements.
For example, 0011,101,\texttt{0011}, \texttt{101},0011,101, and 10010\texttt{10010}10010 are good binary strings, while 0010\texttt{0010}0010 and 101001\texttt{101001}101001 are not good.

Define the goodness of a binary string SSS, denoted f(S)f(S)f(S), to be the length of the longest contiguous substring‡^\ddagger‡ of SSS that's good.

You're given a binary string SSS of length NNN. Compute the sum of goodness across all of its contiguous substrings.

That is, compute

∑i=1N∑j=iNf(SiSi+1…Sj)\sum_{i=1}^N \sum_{j=i}^N f(S_iS_{i+1}\ldots S_j)i=1∑N​j=i∑N​f(Si​Si+1​…Sj​)†^\dagger† A palindrome is a string that reads the same forward and backward. 0,101,00\texttt{0}, \texttt{101}, \texttt{00}0,101,00 are examples of palindromes, while 01\texttt{01}01 and 0100\texttt{0100}0100 are not.

**In particular, note that the empty string is considered to be a palindrome.**

‡^\ddagger‡ A substring of a string SSS is obtained by deleting some (maybe zero) characters from the prefix of SSS and some (maybe zero) characters from the suffix of SSS.

For example, if S=1011S = \texttt{1011}S=1011, some examples of its substrings are 0,1,101,11\texttt{0}, \texttt{1}, \texttt{101}, \texttt{11}0,1,101,11. However, 111\texttt{111}111 is not a substring of SSS.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of the binary string SSS.
The second line contains the binary string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of goodness across all substrings of SSS.
constraints: SSS is a binary string, i.e. Si∈{0,1}S_i \in \{0, 1\}Si​∈{0,1}.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The substrings of SSS, and their associated goodness values, are:

1\texttt{1}1: goodness is 111, appears twice.
0\texttt{0}0: goodness is 111, appears once.
10\texttt{10}10: goodness is 111, appears once.
01\texttt{01}01: goodness is 111, appears once.
101\texttt{101}101: goodness is 333, appears once.

So, the sum over all substrings is 1⋅2+1+1+1+3=81\cdot 2 + 1 + 1 + 1 + 3 = 81⋅2+1+1+1+3=8.
Test case 222: Every substring is a palindrome, and hence has goodness equal to its length. The answer is the sum of all substring lengths which is 202020.
